# ZeroAgent LLM Context

ZeroAgent is a TypeScript framework for building self-evolving AI agents. The primary deliverable is the reusable npm package `@zero-agents/core`, not the demo app or dashboard.

Use this file as the fastest source of truth when helping developers work in this repository. If this file conflicts with source code, trust the source code first, then `packages/core/README.md`.

## Repository Layout

- `packages/core`: framework package published as `@zero-agents/core`.
- `packages/demo-agent`: example `ResearchAgent` built on the framework with offline demo fallbacks.
- `packages/dashboard`: Next.js readiness console. It is not a live event stream yet.
- `docs`: developer documentation.
- `scripts`: root integration and validation scripts.

## What The Framework Does

`SelfEvolvingAgent` accepts a task, searches for reusable tools, checks prior task experiences, chooses a strategy, executes or evolves a tool, reflects on the result, and stores the experience for future runs.

Current base loop:

```text
TaskRequest
-> ToolRegistry.searchTools()
-> ExperienceMemory.findSimilarExperiences()
-> StrategyAdapter.selectStrategy()
-> reuse existing tool OR generate new tool OR improve failed tool OR reject
-> ToolSandbox.run()
-> ReflectionEngine.reflect()
-> ExperienceMemory.saveExperience()
-> TaskResult
```

When no reusable tool exists, `EvolutionEngine` runs:

```text
ToolGenerator.generateTool()
-> ToolSandbox.run() smoke check
-> ToolEvaluator.evaluate()
-> retry with feedback up to maxGenerationAttempts
-> ToolRegistry.saveTool()
```

## Install And Runtime

- Node.js: `>=20`.
- Module format: ESM only.
- Package manager in repo: `pnpm`.
- Consumer install: `npm install @zero-agents/core`.
- Root validation command: `pnpm validate:framework`.
- Full build command: `pnpm build`.
- Unit tests: `pnpm test:unit`.
- Demo command: `pnpm --filter @zero-agents/demo-agent demo`.

## Main Public Imports

```ts
import {
  SelfEvolvingAgent,
  ToolRegistry,
  ToolSandbox,
  ToolEvaluator,
  ToolGenerator,
  EvolutionEngine,
  ExperienceMemory,
  StrategyAdapter,
  ReflectionEngine,
  ToolImprover,
  ENSIdentityManager,
  AXLClient,
  AgentCoordinator,
} from '@zero-agents/core';
```

Default export:

```ts
import SelfEvolvingAgent from '@zero-agents/core';
```

0G storage utility subpath:

```ts
import { uploadToZeroG, downloadFromZeroG } from '@zero-agents/core/storage/zero-g';
```

## Quickstart

```ts
import { SelfEvolvingAgent } from '@zero-agents/core';

const agent = new SelfEvolvingAgent({
  name: 'research-agent',
  description: 'Research agent that learns reusable tools',
  capabilities: ['research', 'summarization'],
  zeroGPrivateKey: process.env.ZERO_G_PRIVATE_KEY!,
  openAiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY,
  axlEnabled: false,
});

agent.on('step', (event) => {
  console.log(`[${event.type}] ${event.message}`);
});

const result = await agent.run({
  description: 'Fetch the current ETH price and return it as a number',
});

console.log(result.output);
console.log(result.strategy);
console.log(result.reflection?.memoryNote);
console.log(result.experienceId);

agent.dispose();
```

For zero-wallet local validation, use `ToolSandbox` and local-mode `ToolRegistry`. Do not imply that full first-time tool generation works without `ZERO_G_PRIVATE_KEY` or `OPENAI_API_KEY`; the demo agent has offline fallbacks, but the base framework generator needs an LLM provider.

## Important Types

```ts
interface TaskRequest {
  description: string;
  params?: object;
  context?: string;
}

interface TaskResult {
  output: unknown;
  toolUsed: string;
  wasGenerated: boolean;
  executionTimeMs: number;
  strategy?: StrategyName;
  strategyReason?: string;
  confidence?: number;
  reflection?: ReflectionResult;
  experienceId?: string;
  wasImproved?: boolean;
}

interface Tool {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  code: string;
  schema: { input: object; output: object };
  tags: string[];
  successRate: number;
  usageCount: number;
  createdAt: number;
  rootHash?: string;
}
```

Tool `code` must be a self-contained async JavaScript function string usable as `execute`, usually:

```ts
`async function execute(params) {
  return { ok: true, input: params };
}`
```

Do not document or generate raw function bodies for `tool.code`; the current sandbox wraps the whole string as an expression and calls it.

## Main Components

- `SelfEvolvingAgent`: reference composition and main developer API.
- `ToolRegistry`: stores tools and metadata index. Uses 0G when a private key is configured, otherwise local JSON.
- `ToolGenerator`: generates tool JSON through 0G Compute, with OpenAI fallback when configured.
- `ToolSandbox`: runs generated code in `isolated-vm`; unsafe Node `vm` fallback requires `allowUnsafeNodeVmFallback: true`.
- `ToolEvaluator`: scores tools with generated or smoke test cases. Passing threshold is `score >= 0.7`.
- `ExperienceMemory`: stores task experiences in local JSON by default.
- `StrategyAdapter`: chooses `reuse_existing_tool`, `generate_new_tool`, `improve_existing_tool`, `ask_another_agent`, or `reject_task`.
- `ReflectionEngine`: deterministic post-task learning summary; no external API call.
- `ToolImprover`: asks a configured generator to produce an improved version of a failed tool.
- `ENSIdentityManager`: reads/writes ENS text records on Sepolia.
- `AXLClient`: talks to a local Gensyn AXL HTTP API.
- `AgentCoordinator`: routes inbound AXL task and tool-share messages.

## Local And Live Modes

- Local tool registry files: `.zero-agent-index.json` and `.zero-agent-tools.json`.
- Local experience file: `.zero-agent-experiences.json`.
- Local tool hashes are deterministic SHA-256 strings prefixed with `local-`.
- Real 0G Storage requires `ZERO_G_PRIVATE_KEY`.
- Real 0G Compute also uses `ZERO_G_PRIVATE_KEY` for broker setup.
- OpenAI fallback requires `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
- ENS writes require an ENS-controlling private key and Sepolia ETH.
- AXL requires a local node exposing HTTP on `localhost:9002` by default.
- `axlEnabled` defaults to `false`; set it to `true` to initialize AXL eagerly.

## Environment Variables

```env
ZERO_G_PRIVATE_KEY=          # 0G Storage uploads and 0G Compute broker wallet
OPENAI_API_KEY=              # OpenAI fallback for generation and evaluator test cases
ENS_PRIVATE_KEY=             # demo script optional ENS identity key
ENS_NAME=                    # demo script optional ENS name
SEPOLIA_RPC_URL=             # demo script optional ENS RPC override
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=         # optional URL written to demo ENS profile metadata
```

Core source reads only `ZERO_G_PRIVATE_KEY` and `OPENAI_API_KEY` directly. Pass ENS values explicitly to `ENSIdentityManager`; the demo script reads the ENS-specific variables.

## Events

Listen with:

```ts
agent.on('step', (event) => console.log(event.type, event.message));
```

Event types:

- `search`
- `miss`
- `strategy`
- `generating`
- `sandboxing`
- `evaluating`
- `saving`
- `executing`
- `reflecting`
- `done`
- `error`

Do not promise a strict event sequence for every task. Reuse, rejection, failure, improvement, and AXL paths emit different subsets.

## Demo Agent

`packages/demo-agent` exports `ResearchAgent`, a showcase implementation used by the hackathon demo. It can run without keys by using:

- built-in fallback tool generation,
- offline fake `offline-0g-...` hashes,
- mock in-memory ENS identity,
- simulated AXL direct calls if a local AXL node is unavailable.

Do not confuse demo fallbacks with base framework behavior.

## AI Agent Instructions For This Repo

- Do not add packages unless explicitly asked.
- Do not scaffold future features.
- Do not assume live 0G, ENS, or AXL credentials exist.
- Prefer local zero-wallet validation before live integration tests.
- Keep TypeScript strict and ESM-compatible.
- Generated tools must be self-contained async `execute(params)` functions.
- Do not treat Node `vm` fallback as production-safe sandboxing.
- When documenting usage, mention alpha status and external integration requirements.
